source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, the lines $y=-x+b$ and $y=kx$ are represented in the same Cartesian coordinate system. What is the solution set of the inequality $-x+b<kx$ with respect to $x$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} From the graph, it is known that the graphs of the two lines intersect at ($-1$, $-3$), \\
$\because$ $-x+b<kx$, \\
$\therefore$ select the part to the right of the intersection point, \\
thus, for $x>-1$, $-x+b<kx$, \\
so the answer is: $\boxed{x>-1}$

answer_text describes two reasoning steps: identify intersection at x=-1, then choose the region to the right
this is a simple but genuine image-plus-algebra reasoning chain
verifiability: final answer is a clear set statement: x>-1
the claimed boundary can be checked against the stated intersection x=-1
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support downstream checking
